e95cd34.title: The Ledgers Remember
e95cd34.intro: After the Concord audit wiped the forged ledgers clean, we spent the week making sure the new ones actually stick. Your browser is no longer the bookkeeper — and this patch is the part where everything you earn, spend, pray, and heal remembers you when you log back in.
items: Combat experience — every swing, shot, and spell that lands XP is granted by the server and written to your save. Refresh the page and your Strength still knows what you did.
Prayer — toggling a prayer, draining points, recharging at an altar: all server-side now. Active prayers survive a reload; running dry still clears them, as it should.
Healing — the Aldgate fountain, Dr. Ticksworth's chair, and Glen's controversial cracker all heal through the server. That extra hitpoint is real chalk in the health ledger, not a browser fantasy.
Inventory — burying bones, eating food, and every other debit now targets the slot you actually used. No more phantom removals from the wrong pocket.
The Exchange, trading, and guild vaults — when the server rewrites your save mid-session, the client pulls the authoritative copy instead of clobbering it on the next autosave.
New adventurers — starter kit is defined by the server. Twenty-five coins in the bank, tools in the pack, glock on the hip: the same honest morning everyone gets.
e95cd34.signoff: The vale keeps its chalk. See you on the road. — The Larpscape team
